texto: Whence then come my errors? texto: I suppose therefore that all things I see are illusions; I believe that nothing has ever existed of everything my lying memory tells me. I think I have no senses. texto: Some years ago I was struck by the large number of falsehoods that I had accepted as true in my childhood, and by the highly doubtful nature of the whole edifice that I had subsequently based on them. I realized that it was necessary, once in the course of my life, to demolish everything completely and start again right from the foundations if I wanted to establish anything at all in the sciences that was stable and likely to last.

texto: Thus the perception of the infinite is somehow prior in me to the perception of the finite, that is, my perception of God is prior to my perception of myself. For how would I understand that I doubt and that I desire, that is, that I lack something and that I am not wholly perfect, unless there were some idea in me of a more perfect being, by comparison with which I might recognize my defects?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de René Descartes que provoca temas ligados a fe. Ao questionar “Thus the perception of the infinite is somehow prior in me to the perception of the finite, that …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre fe, finitude, incompleteness, com foco em incompleteness e infinity.
